title: Idempotent Queue Consumer
description: Idempotent consumption with dedupe keys, retries/backoff, and poison-message handling for at-least-once delivery.

cost.costNotes: Seeded via heuristics; validate against current pricing and expected workload (throughput, storage, retention, egress).

provenance.estimatedMonthlyRangeUsd: Idempotent queue consumer requires: (1) Message queue infrastructure (SQS: $0.40/million requests, Azure Queue: $0.03/10k ops, or self-hosted RabbitMQ/Kafka on VMs), (2) Deduplication storage (Redis/DynamoDB for tracking message IDs: $20-500/month), (3) Dead-letter/poison message queue storage ($5-100/month), (4) Compute for consumers (serverless functions: $10-500/month, or dedicated instances: $20-1500/month). Min represents low-throughput workload with managed queues (10M msgs/month, small Redis, Lambda functions). Max represents high-throughput production (1B+ msgs/month, Redis cluster for dedup store, dedicated consumer instances, multi-region setup with cross-region data transfer).
provenance.adoptionCost: Medium-high complexity implementation requiring: (1) Deduplication logic design and key strategy (4-8 hours), (2) Idempotency storage implementation with TTL management (8-16 hours), (3) Retry mechanism with exponential backoff implementation (8-12 hours), (4) Poison message detection and dead-letter queue handling (6-10 hours), (5) Testing scenarios including duplicates, partial failures, concurrent processing (12-20 hours), (6) Monitoring and observability setup for duplicate rates, retry metrics (6-8 hours), (7) Documentation of deduplication strategy and operational runbooks (4-6 hours). Total: 48-80 hours at $50-75/hour blended rate with AI assistance = $2400-6000. Mid-point reflects typical implementation with some existing queue infrastructure but new idempotency layer.
provenance.licenseCost: Pattern uses open-source message queue systems (RabbitMQ, Apache Kafka, NATS) or pay-per-use cloud services (AWS SQS, Azure Queue Storage, GCP Pub/Sub). Deduplication storage typically uses open-source Redis, or cloud-native services (DynamoDB, Azure Table Storage) with usage-based pricing. No proprietary licenses required. Commercial message queue support (Confluent, CloudAMQP) or enterprise Redis (Redis Enterprise) are optional add-ons, not required for pattern implementation.

provides.reasoning: Core capability of this pattern - ensures messages are processed exactly once semantically even with at-least-once delivery guarantees through deduplication keys.
Pattern explicitly implements retry logic with backoff for failed message processing, providing resilient consumption behavior.
Pattern includes poison-message handling as stated in description; this is a key capability for production-ready queue consumers that prevents stuck messages from blocking processing.
Pattern is specifically designed to handle at-least-once delivery semantics safely through idempotency mechanisms, making this delivery guarantee safe for consumers.
Idempotent consumer pattern requires a dead-letter queue (DLQ) for poison message handling after max retry exhaustion — this pattern implements and configures DLQ behavior.
Idempotent consumption requires a deduplication store (Redis, DynamoDB) to track processed message IDs and prevent duplicate processing.

requires.reasoning: Fundamental dependency - this is a queue consumer pattern that requires a message queue infrastructure to consume messages from.
To implement idempotent consumption with dedupe keys, pattern requires persistent storage (database, cache, or key-value store) to track processed message IDs/keys across restarts and failures.

supports_nfr.reason: Pattern supports optional audit logging for message processing events and deduplication operations.
Pattern is compatible with GDPR requirements through proper message and deduplication data retention policies.
Pattern supports HIPAA compliance when queue and deduplication store are properly secured and audited.
Pattern is compatible with CCPA requirements for message data processing and retention management.
Pattern supports SOX compliance with appropriate audit logging and message durability guarantees.
Deduplication key lookups can support read-your-writes consistency when required by the store implementation.
At-least-once delivery with persistent queue and deduplication store enforces strict durability guarantees.

supports_constraints.reason: Pattern implements idempotent consumption to safely handle at-least-once delivery; incompatible with at-most-once (no dedup needed) or exactly-once (transactional approach required).

requires_constraints.reason: Idempotent queue consumer pattern is cloud-agnostic and works with any cloud provider or on-premises infrastructure.
Queue consumer pattern can be implemented in any backend programming language.
Queue consumers typically run as background workers, batch processors, data pipelines, or async API handlers.
Pattern is compatible with optional caching for deduplication lookups or message processing optimization.
At-least-once delivery requires async messaging infrastructure. Setting messaging_delivery_guarantee without enabling async_messaging is a logical contradiction.
Pattern requires a key-value store or similar fast lookup mechanism for deduplication key tracking.
Pattern supports both multi-tenant and single-tenant queue consumer deployments.
Queue consumers can process messages individually or in batches depending on implementation strategy.
Pattern can participate in distributed transactions or operate with simpler idempotency-based guarantees.
Pattern works with both real-time streaming queues and traditional message queue implementations.
Pattern is independent of vector search capabilities in the application domain.
Pattern works regardless of whether document store is used for message data or deduplication storage.
Pattern is independent of graph database usage in the application domain.
Pattern is independent of time-series database usage in the application domain.
Queue consumers can process OLTP workloads or other workload types asynchronously via message queue.
Queue consumers can process OLAP workloads or other workload types asynchronously via message queue.

requires_nfr.reason: Queue-based async processing with deduplication store dependency limits maximum availability to four nines.
Queue polling, deduplication lookup, and async processing introduce minimum 100ms p95 latency floor.
Retry attempts with exponential backoff and deduplication checks cause p99 latency floor of 500ms.
Automated retry with exponential backoff enables RTO of at least 1 minute for recovery.
At-least-once delivery guarantees zero data loss, supporting RPO of 0 minutes or better.
Pattern scales to 10M jobs/hour ceiling with horizontal consumer scaling and parallelism.

deduplication_strategy.description: How to identify duplicate messages. message_id uses queue-provided ID, content_hash computes hash of message body, custom_key uses application-defined deduplication key from message attributes.

deduplication_ttl_seconds.description: How long to remember processed message IDs for deduplication. Longer TTL prevents more duplicates but uses more storage. Typical range: 1 hour to 24 hours.

max_retry_attempts.description: Maximum number of times to retry a failed message before marking as poison. 0 disables retries, 3-5 is typical for transient failures.

backoff_strategy.description: Retry delay strategy. Exponential (2^n) prevents thundering herd, linear increases steadily, fixed uses constant delay.

initial_backoff_seconds.description: Initial delay before first retry. Lower values retry faster but may overwhelm failing dependencies.

max_backoff_seconds.description: Maximum delay between retries for exponential/linear backoff. Caps retry delays to prevent indefinite waiting.

poison_message_threshold.description: Number of receive attempts before treating message as poison. Should be higher than max_retry_attempts to account for visibility timeouts.

poison_message_action.description: What to do with poison messages. dead_letter_queue moves to DLQ for investigation, log_and_delete removes permanently with logging, alert_and_quarantine triggers alerts and stores separately.

visibility_timeout_seconds.description: How long message is hidden from other consumers while processing. Should exceed typical processing time to prevent duplicate processing.

batch_size.description: Number of messages to fetch per poll. Higher batches improve throughput but increase blast radius of failures and memory usage.

warn_nfr.reason: At <10 jobs/hour, queue-based async processing with deduplication overhead is not justified; synchronous processing is simpler.
warn_nfr.message: queue-consumer-idempotency: peak_jobs_per_hour is {actual} (<10). Queue infrastructure and deduplication overhead likely outweighs benefits; consider synchronous processing.
